all.label: Install all Traigent skills
all.secondary: Installs all published user-facing Traigent SDK skills from the dedicated public repo.

entrypoints.label: Entry points only
entrypoints.command: npx skills add Traigent/traigent-skills --skill traigent --skill traigent-quickstart
entrypoints.secondary: Useful only when you want the shortest local skill footprint.

goals.label: Integrate the decorator
promptSteps: Inspect my target function and add the @traigent.optimize decorator without changing behavior.
Define the inputs, objective, and eval data needed for a safe optimization run.

goals.label: Find the knobs that matter
promptSteps: Map the tunable variables and constraints before running optimization.
After the run, identify the significant tuned variables and explain which config changed the outcome.

goals.label: Set up evaluation
promptSteps: Build or validate an eval dataset and scoring function before any paid run.
Show me the pass/fail criteria that make the optimization result trustworthy.

goals.label: Run optimization
promptSteps: Run the full flow in mock or dry-run mode first, with no API keys and no LLM cost.
Only after the dry run passes, run the real optimization and track cost, trials, and final score.

goals.label: Analyze and apply results
promptSteps: Read the optimization results, compare the winning config against baseline, and explain the tradeoffs.
Apply the selected config as a small code change and add a regression check.

goals.label: Debug a run
promptSteps: Inspect the failed run, logs, traces, and config space before changing code.
Fix the smallest root cause, then rerun the mock path before retrying a real run.

goals.label: Integrate a framework
promptSteps: Adapt the setup for my framework, such as LangChain, LiteLLM, DSPy, or a direct SDK call.
Keep the framework wrapper testable so optimization can be validated locally before deployment.
